zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Hay -III Lord Yester於1485年11月出生在蘇格蘭哈丁頓史爾也斯特也斯特 卡素時，他的父親John Hay of Wester Hoprew, latterly of Snaid - 2nd Lord Hay of Yester - Provost of Dundee21歲，母親Elizabeth Crichton Of Kinnoull20歲。他於1505年與Elizabeth Douglas of  Angus在蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1543年7月19日在英國諾圖波藍德布藍斯頓享年57歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭哈丁頓史爾也斯特也斯特 卡素。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John Hay -III Lord Yester于1485年11月出生在苏格兰哈丁頓史爾也斯特也斯特 卡素时，他的父亲John Hay of Wester Hoprew, latterly of Snaid - 2nd Lord Hay of Yester - Provost of Dundee21岁，母亲Elizabeth Crichton Of Kinnoull20岁。他于1505年与Elizabeth Douglas of  Angus在苏格兰阿爾迪史爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1543年7月19日在英国諾圖波藍德布藍斯頓享年57岁，埋葬在苏格兰哈丁頓史爾也斯特也斯特 卡素。

nameMeanings.name: Hay
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English (northern; of Norman origin): habitational name from any of several places in Normandy called La Haie or La Haye (Old French haie ‘hedge, enclosure’, ‘forest for hunting deer and other animals’, a borrowing of the ancient Germanic word haga). Robert de Haia or de la Haye is known to have come from La Haye-du-Puits in Manche; he was the founder of Boxgrove Priory in Sussex (1123), and holder of the Honor of Halnaker (Sussex) and (by marriage) the barony of Kolswein (Lincolnshire). The Norman name was also taken to Ireland, where it has since flourished in the county of Wexford as Hay and Hayes . Elsewhere in Ireland the name usually has a native Irish origin, see below.

English: topographic name from Middle English hay(e), heye, heghe ‘enclosure’ (Old English (ge)hæg) or ‘forest fenced off for hunting’ (Old French haie); or else a habitational name from a place so called, such as Hay (in Herefordshire and Westmorland) or Hey in Scammonden (Yorkshire). It was no doubt sometimes synonymous with Hayward .
English: nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, hey, high ‘high, tall’ (Old English hēah).

variants.name: High
meanings: English:
nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, hey, high ‘high, tall’ (Old English hēah). Compare Hay 3.
topographic name for someone who lived ‘(on the) height or hill’, from Middle English heigh ‘height, hilltop’ (Old English hēah). Compare Hight .

variants.name: Hai
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Abdul, Syed, Arifa, Atta, Faiz, Hamid, Iftekhar, Iskandar, Mohamed, Mohammad, Mohannad.  Vietnamese Mui, Pham, Tran, Vo, Bui, Cao, Chau, Dinh, Duong, Nguyen, Sau, Vo Van.  Jewish Ilan, Ronen, Shlomo.  Chinese Chan, Chiming, Lung, Wai Fun, Yin.
Muslim (mainly the Indian subcontinent): from a personal name based on Arabic ḥayy ‘alive’. Al-Ḥayy ‘the Living’ is an attribute of Allah.
Jewish: variant of   Hay  .

variants.name: Dehay
meanings: French: topographic name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, for someone who lived by an enclosure, from the masculine form of Old French haye ‘hedge’; or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word (see Hay 9).
Altered form (also De Hay) of Dutch De Haij or De Haaij, which is probably of French origin (see above).

variants.name: Haig
meanings: Scottish and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of various places in northern France named with Old Norse hagi ‘enclosure’, a word with cognates in most ancient Germanic languages. Compare Hay . The Haigs of Bemersyde in Berwickshire derive their name from the district of La Hague in Manche.
Scottish and Irish (Cavan): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Thaoig and Mac Thaidhg (see McCaig ).

variants.name: Hayman
meanings: English: topographic name probably from Middle English hai(e), hei(e) ‘enclosure’ (Old English (ge)hæg), denoting a dweller by an enclosure (see Hay 2 ), or else a nickname for a ‘keeper of the enclosure’ (compare Hayward ).
English: nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, high(e) (Old English hēah); see also Hay 3.
English: occupational name for a seller of hay, from Middle English hai, hei (Old English hēg, hīg).

variants.name: Hayes
meanings: Irish (Cork): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hAodha ‘descendant of Aodh’, a personal name meaning ‘fire’. Compare McCoy . In some cases especially in County Wexford, the surname is of English origin (see below), having been taken to Ireland by the Normans.
English: variant of Hay , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

English: topographic name from the plural form of Middle English hay(e), heye, heghe ‘enclosure’ (see Hay ), sometimes used as a collective noun for a farm, especially in Devon, where it is a frequent minor placename. Compare Hain .

variants.name: Haycock
meanings: English: from a pet form of the Middle English personal name Hai + coc; see Hay 4 and compare Cocke .

variants.name: Haylett
meanings: English (Norfolk): from a pet form (double diminutive) of the personal name Hai (see Hay 4), from Hai + -el + -ot.

variants.name: Hey
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of   Hay  .
Dutch: variant, mostly Americanized (or archaic) and Flemish, of Heij, a topographic name for someone who lived on a heath, Dutch hei, heide.
German: metonymic occupational name for a grower or mower of grass, from Middle High German höu ‘grass, hay’.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Hay -III Lord Yester was born in November 1485, in Yester Castle, Yester, Haddingtonshire, Scotland, his father, John Hay of Wester Hoprew, latterly of Snaid - 2nd Lord Hay of Yester - Provost of Dundee, was 21 and his mother, Elizabeth Crichton Of Kinnoull, was 20. He married Elizabeth Douglas of  Angus in 1505, in Aberdeenshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 19 July 1543, in Branxton, Northumberland, England, at the age of 57, and was buried in Yester Castle, Yester, Haddingtonshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John Hay -III Lord Yester est né en novembre 1485, à Yester Castle, Yester, Haddingtonshire, Écosse, son père, John Hay of Wester Hoprew, latterly of Snaid - 2nd Lord Hay of Yester - Provost of Dundee, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Crichton Of Kinnoull, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Douglas of  Angus en 1505, à Aberdeenshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 19 juillet 1543, à Branxton, Northumberland, Angleterre, à l’âge de 57 ans, et a été enterré à Yester Castle, Yester, Haddingtonshire, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John Hay -III Lord Yester im November 1485 in Yester Castle, Yester, Haddingtonshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Hay of Wester Hoprew, latterly of Snaid - 2nd Lord Hay of Yester - Provost of Dundee 21 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Crichton Of Kinnoull 20. Er hat 1505 in Aberdeenshire, Scotland Elizabeth Douglas of  Angus geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 19. Juli 1543 im Alter von 57 Jahren in Branxton, Northumberland, England und wurde in Yester Castle, Yester, Haddingtonshire, Scotland begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John Hay -III Lord Yesterが1485年11月にスコットランドハッドイントンシュアイアイエスターイエスター カストルで生まれた時，父John Hay of Wester Hoprew, latterly of Snaid - 2nd Lord Hay of Yester - Provost of Dundeeは21歳，母Elizabeth Crichton Of Kinnoullは20歳であった。1505年にスコットランドアバーディーンシュアイアでElizabeth Douglas of  Angusと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1543年7月19日にイギリスノースウンバーランドブランクストンで57歳で死去し，スコットランドハッドイントンシュアイアイエスターイエスター カストルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Hay -III Lord Yester(이)가 1485년 11월 스컷랜드 하딩토실르 예스터 예스터 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Hay of Wester Hoprew, latterly of Snaid - 2nd Lord Hay of Yester - Provost of Dundee(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Crichton Of Kinnoull(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1505년 스컷랜드 압르디실르에서 Elizabeth Douglas of  Angus(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1543년 7월 19일 영국 노르스벌랜드 브랜스턴에서 57세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 하딩토실르 예스터 예스터 카스틀에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John Hay -III Lord Yester родился ноябрь 1485 г. в местности иестир кастл, иестир, хаддингтоншир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени John Hay of Wester Hoprew, latterly of Snaid - 2nd Lord Hay of Yester - Provost of Dundee было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Crichton Of Kinnoull, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Douglas of  Angus в 1505 г. в местности абирдиншир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 19 июля 1543 г. в местности бранкстон, нортумбирланд, Англия в возрасте 57 лет и был похоронен в местности иестир кастл, иестир, хаддингтоншир, Шотландия.

Se casó con Elizabeth Douglas of  Angus en 1505, en Aberdeenshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 19 de julio de 1543, en Branxton, Northumberland, Inglaterra, a la edad de 57 años, y fue sepultado en Yester Castle, Yester, Haddingtonshire, Scotland.
